metadata.notes: 2/1 Game Force: strong 1NT (15-17), 5-card majors, 2/1 responses game forcing, weak 2s in all suits, Stayman, Jacoby transfers, Jacoby 2NT, standard Blackwood, 2nd/4th leads, standard attitude signals.

leads.method: Top of sequence (A from AK), 4th highest from honour suits, top of doubleton
4th highest from longest and strongest suit; top of internal sequence (J from KJ10x)
2nd highest from bad suits (no honour), 4th highest from suits headed by an honour

signals.method: Standard attitude: high card encourages, low card discourages
Standard count: high-low shows even, low-high shows odd
signals.context: Count signal when attitude is known or declarer leads

signals.method: High card = preference for higher suit, low card = lower suit
signals.context: Suit preference when giving ruff or in obvious situations

discards.method: Standard attitude: high card in a suit encourages that suit
Standard count when attitude already shown

nt-responses.name: 1NT Responses
nt-responses.description: Responses to 1NT (15-17): Stayman, Jacoby transfers, invitational and game bids

meaning.description: Stayman — asks for 4-card major, 8+ HCP or weak hand with both majors
4 spades, 4+ hearts — pick a major, game forcing
Game values, no heart fit — to play 3NT
Game values, no spade fit — to play 3NT
Jacoby transfer to hearts — 5+ hearts, any strength
Exactly 5 hearts, offers choice of game, 10+ HCP
Jacoby transfer to spades — 5+ spades, any strength
Exactly 5 spades, offers choice of game, 10+ HCP
Minor suit Stayman — 4-4 in minors, slam interest or two weak minors
GF, 5+ clubs — game force or slam try
No major stopper, no 4-card fit, minimum
Stoppers everywhere, no special club fit

blackwood.name: Standard Blackwood
blackwood.description: 4NT asks for aces. 5C=0/4, 5D=1, 5H=2, 5S=3. Follow-up 5NT asks for kings.

major-raises.name: Major Suit Raises (2/1)
major-raises.description: After 1H or 1S opening. Forcing 1NT, limit raises, Jacoby 2NT, splinters. 2/1 in a new suit at 2-level is GF.

meaning.description: Forcing 1NT — 6-12 HCP, no fit (or 3-card limit raise planning 1NT then 3M)
Rebid of major — 5+ cards, minimum (12-14 HCP)
Strong rebid of major — 6+ cards, 15-17 HCP
Jacoby 2NT — 4+ card support, game forcing, slam interest
Singleton or void in spades (if hearts opened)
Singleton or void in hearts (if spades opened)
Strong 5-5 or extra values, second suit
Splinter — 4+ support, GF, singleton/void in clubs
Splinter — 4+ support, GF, singleton/void in diamonds
Limit raise — 3-card support, 10-12 HCP (or 4-card limit raise via forcing 1NT)
To play — 4-5 card support, 7-10 HCP, pre-emptive raise
3+ clubs (may be short), balanced 12-14/18-19 or unbalanced, denies 5-card major or 4+ diamonds if balanced
2/1 GF — 5+ clubs, 12+ HCP (not if passed hand)
Minimum balanced, 12-14 HCP, no spade fit
Forcing 1NT — 6-12 HCP, no fit, may have 3 hearts
5+ hearts, no extra, minimum — sets hearts as trump candidate
Club raise — 4+ clubs, fit confirmed, 12-15 HCP
Balanced 18-19 HCP, stoppers in all suits
5+ hearts, minimum — sets hearts as trump candidate
Diamond raise — 4+ diamonds, fit confirmed, 12-15 HCP
Splinter — 4+ hearts, GF, singleton/void in clubs
Splinter — 4+ hearts, GF, singleton/void in diamonds
Splinter — 4+ spades, GF, singleton/void in clubs
Splinter — 4+ spades, GF, singleton/void in diamonds
Splinter — 4+ spades, GF, singleton/void in hearts
Balanced: no 5-card major, no singleton/void, 15-17 HCP
Strong, artificial, forcing — 22+ balanced or 8.5+ tricks unbalanced
Waiting — 0-7 HCP or any GF hand

meaningOverride.description: Weak two — 6+ diamonds, wider range in 3rd seat

meaning.description: Ogust — asks about hand quality and suit quality
Minimum hand, good suit (two of top three honours)

meaningOverride.description: Weak two — 6+ hearts, wider range in 3rd seat

meaning.description: Ogust — asks about hand quality and suit quality

meaningOverride.description: Weak two — 6+ spades, wider range in 3rd seat

meaning.description: Ogust — asks about hand quality and suit quality
Puppet Stayman — asks for 5-card major or 4-card major
Pass or correct — find the solid suit
